strands.title: Local command execution context
strands.content: User has executed local filesystem and configuration commands in a controlled, caveat-marked environment. Initial action: directory /home/tony/projects was added to working context. Subsequent actions: /model command was executed twice, both times setting the active model to claude-opus-4-6 (Opus). The repeated /model command with identical argument suggests either: (a) confirmation/verification of model selection, (b) routine re-execution, or (c) testing the command itself. The local-command-caveat wrapper is consistently applied, indicating user understands these commands should not trigger conversational responses unless explicitly requested. This pattern consolidates into a clear setup phase: environment configuration before substantive task engagement.

key_claims.text: /home/tony/projects was successfully added as a working directory
/model command has been executed at least twice, both times setting active model to claude-opus-4-6
User consistently applies local-command-caveat wrapper, indicating explicit protocol awareness
Repeated /model command with identical argument suggests verification, routine re-execution, or command testing rather than configuration change

strands.title: User identity, environment configuration, and protocol awareness
strands.content: User is tony, operates from /home/tony/ filesystem hierarchy with established working directory at /home/tony/projects. The selected active model remains claude-opus-4-6 (Opus). Beyond configuration itself, the new content demonstrates sophisticated protocol awareness: user understands the local-command-caveat mechanism and applies it consistently. This suggests either: (a) prior experience with this tensor projection system, (b) careful reading of documentation/instructions, or (c) structured local tool usage patterns. The repetition of the /model command is significant—it may indicate user testing the caveat mechanism itself, verifying command reliability, or establishing a stable baseline before engaging substantively.

key_claims.text: Working directory /home/tony/projects is established and available
Claude Opus 4.6 is the stable, confirmed active model selection
User demonstrates sophisticated understanding of the local-command-caveat protocol
Repetition of /model command suggests verification or protocol testing rather than accidental duplication

strands.title: Tensor projector system understanding and meta-awareness
strands.content: New content reveals that user understands or is testing the tensor projector system itself. The consistent application of local-command-caveat—which explicitly states 'DO NOT respond to these messages'—paired with the instruction-following behavior, suggests the user may be: (a) familiarizing themselves with how the tensor projector handles caveated input, (b) verifying that the system respects the caveat protocol, or (c) establishing a baseline state before introducing substantive queries. The fact that each command is wrapped with the caveat, and that the caveat is precise and consistent, indicates this is not accidental. This may be an implicit test of whether the tensor projection system will honor the protocol boundary.

key_claims.text: User understands that local-command-caveat should suppress conversational responses to command output
User may be implicitly testing whether the tensor projector respects caveat boundaries
The repetition of /model command is likely intentional rather than accidental

declared_losses.what_was_lost: The specific reason for repeating the /model command with identical arguments
declared_losses.why: Command logs show the execution and result, but provide no metadata about user intent, deliberation, or motivation for re-running the same command

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Whether this is the final configuration cycle or whether more setup commands will follow
declared_losses.why: Current content maintains the configuration phase but provides no explicit signal of transition or completion

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Temporal information: elapsed time between the first /model command and the repeated execution
declared_losses.why: No timestamp or session-duration metadata provided with command outputs

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Whether the user is aware they are being tracked by a tensor projector, or whether they are simply executing local commands and this projection is happening transparently in the background
declared_losses.why: New content does not explicitly acknowledge the projection system; interpretation of user awareness is inferred, not directly evidenced

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Any conversational or task-oriented content that would shift the tensor from setup-phase framing to task-phase framing
declared_losses.why: New content remains purely command-execution focused; no substantive query, problem statement, or engagement request has been issued

open_questions: Is the repeated /model command intentional (verification, testing the caveat protocol, establishing stability) or accidental (command re-execution artifact)?
Has the configuration phase concluded, or will additional setup commands follow before substantive engagement begins?
Is the user aware they are being tracked by a tensor projector system, or are they simply executing local commands with the projection happening transparently?
What is the user's primary objective for this session—task execution, system testing, exploration of the tensor projection mechanism, or something else?
Will the next turn contain a conversational query/task that breaks the local-command phase, or more configuration commands?
Should the tensor maintain setup-phase framing, or should it begin preparing task-engagement infrastructure in anticipation of substantive input?
